channel.title: UK Public Affairs & Lobbying Jobs
channel.description: Job listings for Public Affairs roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: An established trade within the financial and professional services sector is seeking a Policy Manager to support its commercial finance agenda. The organisation works closely with industry participants, policy makersand regulators to promote a responsible and well-functioning sector that supports UK businesses and the wider economy. With increasing focus on SME access to finance amid economic and regulatory change, this role offers the opportunity to contribute tohigh-profile policy discussions and engage directly with senior stakeholders across industry and government. Key Responsibilities ‍ Lead on policy issues affecting SME access to finance, including areas such as personal guarantees, credit data and sector-specific lending. Draft consultation responses, policy papers, briefings and board materials with minimal oversight. Support research projects, events and wider delivery of the commercial finance policy programme. Coordinate and contribute to member committees, including preparing agendas and minutes. Engage with government, regulators and industry stakeholders through meetings and roundtables. Build relationships with a broad member base, including banks and specialist lenders. Monitor regulatory and legislative developments, identifying implications and engagement opportunities. Produce member communications including updates, articles and presentations. Skills & Experience ‍ Excellent written skills and proven ability to write policy reports and consultation documents Previous experience in financial services, policy, government, regulation or a membership body environment. Confident engaging with senior stakeholders and representing organisational positions. Strong analytical and research skills, with the ability to interpret complex regulatory material. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍

item.title: Account Director / Senior Account Director (Technology)
item.description: The Organisation Our client is a fast-growing, independent communications agency specialising in technology, investment, and purpose-led organisations. Now in its fourth year, the agency has built a strong reputation for working with “meaningful” organisations across climate, femtech, DEI, financial inclusion, health, and education, alongside some of the most ambitious companies shaping the future of technology. The business has grown revenue by 30% over the last financial year. With a growing international footprint, including expansion into New York, the agency supports a diverse client base spanning start ups,scale ups, investors, and institutions across multiple global markets. Its approach is grounded in thoughtful storytelling, high-quality media engagement, and a genuine respect for journalism. The Opportunity This is a key hire within a high-performing and values-driven agency at a pivotal stage of growth. You will take ownership of key client relationships from the outset, leading delivery across accounts while managing and developing a team. Alongside client work, you will contribute to the broader direction of the business, including input into strategy, operations, and new business activity. The role offers significant scope for impact, responsibility, and progression within a collaborative and intellectually curious environment. Main Responsibilities Lead the strategic direction and delivery of multiple client accounts Build trusted, senior-level relationships with clients across sectors Develop and deliver media strategies that generate high-impact coverage Drive proactive media engagement and maintain strong journalist relationships Manage, mentor, and develop junior team members Contribute to business growth through new business and organic account development Support wider agency initiatives across operations and strategy Skills & Experience Demonstrable experience managing client relationships within a PR agency environment Strong background in technology or technology-adjacent communications Proven ability to secure high-quality, top-tier media coverage Experience leading and developing teams Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of UK, US, and European media landscapes Interest in purpose, emerging technologies, including AI, and their broader impact Commercial awareness with the ability to identify growth opportunities Wider Benefits One-month paid sabbatical after four years’ service Flexible wellbeing support, including fitness or therapy subsidy Generous annual leave, including additional time over the Christmas period and a birthday day off Regular team activities and company-wide events Monthly development check-ins and access to external training Home office and technology provision Access to learning and wellbeing platforms Health-related benefits including eye care support Close To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles at leadingagencies, contact Tasmin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporateaffairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Public Affairs & Policy Manager (Wales) - Not-For-Profit (Health Sector)
item.description: About the Role ‍ Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading not-for-profit organisation in the health sector to appoint a Public Affairs & Policy Manager. This is a key role leading policy and external affairs activity in Wales, with collaboration across UK-wide teams. ‍ The successful candidate will lead public affairs strategy, manage stakeholder relationships, and provide expert political and policy advice to senior leadership. You will oversee a small team and play an important role in shaping the organisation’s external engagement and policy positioning. ‍ This role offers the opportunity to influence health policy, build strong stakeholder relationships, and contribute to meaningful impact across the healthcare system. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ You will lead public affairs and policy activity in Wales, providing strategic insight and advice to internal and external stakeholders. You will build and maintain strong relationships with policymakers, government bodies, and partners across the health sector, ensuring the organisation is positioned as a credible and influential voice. ‍ You will provide high-quality political intelligence, policy analysis, briefings, and advice to senior leaders. The role will involve overseeing policy projects, consultations, stakeholder engagement, and external communications activity, while managing and supporting a small team to deliver high-quality work. Working closely with colleagues across the UK, you will contribute to a joined-up external affairs strategy and help ensure the organisation responds effectively to developments in the political and policy environment. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for someone with a strong understanding of the Welsh political, public affairs, and health policy environment. You will have experience providing policy and strategic advice to senior stakeholders and be confident in building trusted relationships with senior external partners. ‍ You will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce high-quality briefings and policy documents. You will have experience managing complex workstreams and delivering projects to a high standard, as well as the ability to lead or support a team. Welsh language skills are desirable but not essential. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via Ellwood Atfield. Suitable applicants will be invited to an initial briefing call with our team to discuss the role in more detail. ‍ Closing date: Monday 27 April 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged , as shortlisting and interviews will take place on a rolling basis. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Crisis)
item.description: ‍ The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established strategic communications consultancy. They work across a range of sectors, supporting clients through complex and often high-profile situations. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a senior role within a growing and ambitious practice, offering the opportunity to shape and develop crisis and issues communications capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in leading client work, contributing to the broader growth of the practice, and defining areas of specialism within a generalist offer. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the delivery of crisis and issues communications programmes for a range of clients Provide senior strategic counsel on reputational risk, litigation, and high-profile issues Support the development and positioning of the crisis practice within the wider business Build strong, trusted relationships with clients and key external stakeholders Oversee and develop account teams, ensuring high-quality delivery Contribute to new business activity, including pitches and proposals Maintain high standards across client work, ensuring consistency and efficiency Mentor and support the development of junior colleagues ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Strong experience in crisis, issues, and/or litigation communications (essential) Excellent understanding of the media landscape and handling of sensitive situations Proven ability to advise senior stakeholders in complex environments Demonstrated leadership capability with a collaborative approach Commercial awareness, with experience supporting business development activity Ability to operate with sound judgement in fast-paced, high-pressure situations ‍ Benefits ‍ Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Flexible and hybrid working arrangements Generous leave and wellbeing support Structured learning and development opportunities Inclusive, collaborative culture with regular social and team activities ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Associate Director (Financial Services)
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established, mid-sized communications agency with a global offering and a strong heritage in financial services. Known for its agile and collaborative culture, the agency has built a reputation for delivering high-quality, insight-led work, alongside industry recognition for its people-first approach. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a pivotal role within a high-performing financial services team, acting as the number two to the Head of Financial Services. You will play a central role in shaping client strategy, leading delivery across key accounts, and supporting the continued growth of the practice within a nimble and ambitious agency environment. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the strategic direction and delivery of integrated communications programmes for financial services clients Act as a senior counsel to clients, building trusted and long-term relationships Support and deputise for the Head of Financial Services as required Drive new business activity, including contributing to pitches and identifying growth opportunities Oversee and mentor junior team members, fostering a high-performing and supportive team culture Ensure consistently high standards of delivery across multiple accounts Collaborate across the wider agency to deliver integrated, multi-market campaigns Skills & Experience ‍ Significant experience within a communications agency, with a focus on financial services Strong understanding of the financial services landscape, including regulatory and reputational considerations Proven ability to lead client relationships and provide senior strategic counsel Experience in managing and developing high-performing teams Track record of contributing to business development and agency growth Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment Close ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Digital Senior Account Manager
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a fast-growing strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of communications, policy and reputation. With an international footprint, the firm supports senior leaders across government, business and high-profile organisations navigating complex, high-stakes environments. The team is known for its discreet, insight-led approach, combining geopolitical awareness with clear strategic execution. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for a digitally focused communications professional to join a high-performing and entrepreneurial team. You will play a key role in delivering integrated campaigns and digital strategies for clients operating in complex and often high-profile contexts. The role offers significant international exposure, opportunity for regular travel and the chance to work on high-impact mandates with senior stakeholders. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Plan and execute digital communications strategies across sensitive, high-profile client accounts Manage day-to-day social media activity, including drafting, scheduling and publishing content for senior individuals and organisations Develop digital narratives that support reputation management, public positioning and campaign objectives Monitor online conversations, media coverage and stakeholder sentiment, providing real-time insight and recommendations Support crisis and issues management through rapid-response digital content and messaging Conduct digital and stakeholder research, including audience mapping and platform analysis, to inform strategy Analyse campaign performance and produce insight-led reports to refine and optimise activity Work closely with colleagues across strategy, research and public affairs to deliver integrated client programmes Provide hands-on support to senior clients, ensuring digital output is accurate, timely and aligned with wider communications objectives Monitor political, regulatory and media landscapes to inform campaign activity ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Experience in digital communications within an agency, in-house or public sector environment Strong understanding of social media, digital campaigning and online engagement strategies Ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments with sound judgement Experience supporting senior stakeholders or working on campaigns involving complex or sensitive issues Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights Interest in geopolitics and international affairs Excellent written and verbal communication skills Likely to have 3–5 years’ relevant experience – this will not be suitable for someone more experienced and there is no flexibility here ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Content and Digital Communications Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Lead the voice of a major UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact content and social storytelling that drives awareness, engagement, and commercial growth. ‍ If you are energised by building brands through compelling storytelling, strategic content, and high-performing social channels, this role offers the opportunity to make a visible impact. This organisation is at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‍ As Content & Digital Communications Lead, you will own and evolve the organisation’s content marketing and organic social strategy, helping shape how the business communicates across digital channels. You will play a central role in building a scalable always-on content engine that strengthens brand credibility, increases engagement, and supports measurable demand creation across both the core business and new growth propositions. ‍ This is a highly collaborative and fast-moving environment where creativity, strategic thinking, and strong execution are genuinely valued. You will work closely with senior stakeholders, support executive visibility, and create content that influences audiences across the industry. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Own the organisation’s LinkedIn and organic social strategy, driving corporate visibility, thought leadership, proposition education, and growth campaign engagement. Build and manage an always-on social publishing engine, including content series, campaign amplification, publishing cadence, and community engagement strategy. Create high-performing digital content optimised for LinkedIn and other social platforms, using strong storytelling, audience insight, and clear calls-to-action. Support CEO and senior leadership visibility through executive content drafting, voice adaptation, and strategic narrative alignment. Partner closely with Studio, Corporate Communications, and wider marketing teams to deliver integrated multi-channel campaigns and standout creative content. Monitor and optimise social and content performance, using insight and analytics to continuously improve engagement, audience growth, and downstream campaign impact. ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required ‍ Deep expertise in LinkedIn, B2B digital content marketing, and organic social growth strategies. Strong understanding of content performance, audience engagement, and algorithm-informed publishing best practice. Exceptional copywriting and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics into engaging and commercially impactful content. Experience building scalable content programmes, repeatable publishing rhythms, and executive thought leadership content. Proven success growing LinkedIn engagement and visibility for B2B brands and/or senior leadership teams.. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Ability to work collaboratively across marketing, communications, and senior stakeholder groups in a fast-paced environment. ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍ ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Brand Marketing Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Drive the brand strategy of a leading UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact campaigns that build market credibility, awareness, and commercial growth If you are excited by the opportunity to shape brand strategy, launch impactful campaigns, and position a business at the centre of a rapidly evolving industry, this role offers the chance to make a visible and lasting impact. This organisation operates at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‍ As Brand Marketing Manager, you will lead the organisation’s brand marketing strategy and execution, building awareness, credibility, and market preference across priority audiences. You will create and deliver top and mid-funnel campaigns that support new products, vertical market expansion, and long-term brand growth across both the core business and new business acceleration initiatives. ‍ This is a highly collaborative role where you will work closely with Product, Sales, Performance Marketing, Content, and Creative teams to bring brand narratives to life across multiple channels. You will play a key role in establishing market positioning, strengthening category leadership, and building the foundation for measurable commercial growth. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Own the organisation’s brand marketing strategy, roadmap, and execution aligned to strategic priorities, core business growth, and new market expansion. Lead top and mid-funnel campaign strategy and delivery across new propositions, vertical markets, enterprise segments, and category positioning initiatives. Translate brand strategy and value propositions into compelling messaging frameworks, campaign territories, and evidence-led storytelling. Deliver integrated brand-led campaigns that drive awareness and consideration across digital, trade, paid, and earned channels. Own always-on brand activity including thought leadership, market education, and credibility-building campaigns. Partner closely with Product, Growth, Sales, Content, and Creative teams to ensure alignment between brand messaging and commercial priorities. Support campaign execution through clear briefing, stakeholder management, and collaboration with Brand & Creative Studio teams. Track and report on brand and upper-funnel performance including awareness, engagement, message cut-through, and share of voice. ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required ‍ Strong expertise in B2B brand marketing and upper-funnel campaign strategy. Proven ability to build awareness and consideration campaigns for complex products, propositions, or technical services. Strong narrative development, positioning, and messaging capability. Experience launching new products, propositions, or vertical market campaigns. Understanding of how brand and upper-funnel activity supports down streamdemand generation and lead conversion. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Strong stakeholder management, prioritisation, and campaign delivery skills within fast-paced environments. ‍ ‍ ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com.
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director of Communications
item.description: Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with a leading public sector regulatory body that plays a vital role in upholding information rights and data protection standards across the UK. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a rare opportunity for a senior communications leader to join an organisation at a pivotal moment and shape the way it communicates its purpose, decisions and regulatory authority to a wide range of stakeholders. ‍ Reporting to the Executive Director of Communications and Public Affairs and as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director of Communications will set the vision and strategic direction for a modern, high-performing communications function that is embedded early in organisational decision-making and will be leading a multidisciplinary team supported by two direct reports and oversee a team of30. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Set and lead the communications strategy, aligned to organisational priorities and regulatory objectives, working in close partnership with the Executive Director. Position communications as a core strategic function, embedded early in planning, policy and regulatory decision making.. Provide senior counsel to the Executive Team on sensitive, high-profile and reputationally significant issues, leading the organisation’s approach to reputation management and crisis readiness. Champion an insight-driven, outcomes-focused approach to communications, using research, data and evaluation to inform strategy and measure impact. Lead a digital first culture, ensuring channels, content and formats reflect how audiences consume information, while encouraging creativity and innovation. Own and shape the organisation’s overarching narrative and lead proactive engagement to build public trust and confidence. Work with internal communications to align internal and external messaging, supporting leadership communication capability and consistency across the organisation. Lead, inspire and develop a collaborative, high-performing team of around 30 people, fostering a culture of confidence, clarity, accountability and continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Experience Required ‍ Senior leadership experience running a substantial communications function in a high profile, scrutinised environment. Proven experience advising senior leaders on sensitive, complex and reputationally significant communications issues. A demonstrable record of using communications to shape outcomes Experience setting and delivering communications strategy across multiple disciplines, including media, digital, campaigns, stakeholder engagement and events. Strong track record of leading and developing senior managers and building high-performing, confident teams. Experience managing resources and budgets, including commissioning and working with agencies to enhance capacity and capability. Deep understanding of insight-driven, audience-led communications, including digital first approaches and modern communications practice. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.title: Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Lead the UK's foremost voice for brand manufacturers, shaping policy, influencing key stakeholders, and driving the future success of a highly respected membership organisation. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ The British Brands Group (BBG) is the leading voice for brand manufacturers operating in the UK, representing many of the world's most recognisable consumer brands. ‍ We are seeking an outstanding Chief Executive Officer to lead the association, drive member engagement, shape policy and advocacy activity, and ensure BBG continues to deliver exceptional value for its members. ‍ This is a unique opportunity to lead a highly respected membership organisation, working with senior business leaders, policymakers, regulators, and industry stakeholders to influence issues of critical importance to the UK's consumer goods sector. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities: ‍ Lead the overall management and strategic development of BBG, ensuring the organisation operates effectively and delivers against its objectives. Act as Company Secretary, supporting the Chair and Council and ensuring strong governance and compliance. Build and maintain strong relationships with members, government, regulators, trade associations, and other key stakeholders. Lead the development and delivery of BBG's policy, public affairs, and advocacy agenda. Oversee the delivery of member engagement activities, including working groups, events, seminars, and training programmes. Manage the organisation's finances, external suppliers, and day-to-day operations, ensuring long-term sustainability and growth. ‍ ‍ Key Requirements: ‍ Senior leadership experience gained within a trade association, membership body, public affairs consultancy, or similarly stakeholder-led environment. Highly self-motivated with the proven ability to operate independently in a lean organisation. A strong understanding of UK public policy, regulation, and advocacy. Experience supporting boards, councils, or governance structures. Outstanding stakeholder engagement, influencing, and communication skills. Strong commercial, operational, and financial management capability. The credibility and judgement to engage effectively with senior executives, government officials, regulators, and policymakers. ‍ ‍ This is an exceptional opportunity for an experienced and politically astute leader to shape the future of an influential trade body at the heart of the UK's consumer goods sector. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information, please consult the detailed candidate pack and apply with a supporting statement and CV via the form below. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Policy Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an excellent opportunity for a policy professional who enjoys combining analytical thinking with practical, outcomes-focused policy development. ‍ As Senior Policy Officer, you will support the delivery of the College’s policy objectives across key issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publishing, and communications, you will help develop evidence-based policy positions designed to influence government, regulators and NHS bodies. ‍ You will also play an important role in horizon scanning, identifying emerging political, healthcare and regulatory developments, and ensuring the College remains a credible and influential voice on issues impacting surgery and patient care. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Develop evidence-led policy positions across issues impacting surgical practice, workforce and standards. Produce high-quality policy briefings, consultation responses and stakeholder briefings and engagement materials. Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments to identify emerging risks and influencing opportunities. Work closely with campaigns, communications and public affairs colleagues to support wider influencing objectives. Build strong relationships with clinicians, healthcare organisations, regulators and government stakeholders. Support senior stakeholders and College spokespeople with policy advice and external engagement. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Develop Experience working within policy, public affairs or influencing roles in healthcare, government, regulation or membership organisations. Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex information into practical policy recommendations. Excellent written communication skills and experience producing materials for senior audiences. Understanding of political, healthcare or regulatory environments. Strong stakeholder engagement skills and ability to work collaboratively across teams. Comfortable working at pace and managing competing objectives. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: 9:00am Friday 12th June Interview date: Week commencing 22nd June We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍

item.title: Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment a Policy Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a career in health policy and influencing while helping shape the future direction of surgical policy and influencing work at one of the UK’s most respected professional bodies. ‍ As Policy Officer, you will support the development and delivery of policy activity across a wide range of issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publications, and communications, you will monitor and analyse political, NHS and regulatory developments, identify emerging trends and support the development of accessible policy insights and member communications. ‍ The successful candidate will join a collaborative and ambitious team environment with the opportunity to help shape the development of surgical policy, campaigns and member engagement work at one of the UK’s leading professional membership organisations. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments relevant to surgery and dental surgery, identifying emerging issues and opportunities. Work collaboratively with colleagues across campaigns, communications and engagement to support wider influencing objectives. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Experience monitoring policy or political developments and summarising key implications. Strong research and analytical skills, with the ability to synthesise information from multiple sources. Excellent written communication skills, including producing clear briefings and policy updates. Understanding of UK policy processes in healthcare or regulatory environments. Strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and ability to work collaboratively at pace. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. item.title: Engagement Lead (Higher Education)
item.description: About the organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Engagement Lead on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. ‍ The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‍ The opportunity ‍ A key element to the organisation’s growth strategy lies within identifying and leveraging opportunities within the Higher Education sector. As an Engagement Lead, you will play a crucial part in driving expansion; operating as a visible leader for UK university clients. You will optimise brand presence throughout the sector, through partnerships, policy influence and market knowledge. You will also manage a broad range of relationships, engaging with policy teams relating to international student recruitment, cross University partnerships as well as market entry for UK universities. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Establish and maintain client relationships across the higher education sector through proactive engagement. Liaise with clients to understand their issues and strategic needs pertaining to cross country University relations. Promote awareness of launchpad and other services provided by the trade association within the higher education sector. Optimise driving client retention and acquisition through innovative recruitment strategies and market insights. ‍ ‍ Key Skills and Experience Required ‍ Extensive experience in UK Higher Education (preferably in international offices) student recruitment, or partnerships. Strong understanding of sector challenges and solutions for growth in fast-evolving landscapes. Proven ability to quickly build trust withsenior external stakeholders including University Vice-Chancellors and Deans. Able to manage various aspects of stakeholder and client relationships including lead generation, pitching, closure and delivery oversight. Good understanding of HE decision-making as well as student flows and visa compliance obstacles. Adept in conducting qualitative/quantitative research to inform policy reports/briefings on HE trends, and sector reforms. Confident representing the organisation at external events. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Integrated Communications Manager
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a globally respected membership body at the forefront of maritime innovation to recruit anIntegrated Communications Manager. ‍ For more than 160 years, the Royal Institute of Naval Architects (RINA) has advanced the art and science of naval architecture through technical excellence, thought leadership and global collaboration. Today, it brings together professionals across more than 140 countries working across everything from green propulsion and AI to warship resilience and autonomy. ‍ This is a newly created role at the heart of a fast-moving marketing and communications team during a significant period of organisational transformation and growth. ‍ The Integrated Communications Manager will lead integrated email marketing, CRM-led communications and cross-organisational campaign planning -helping grow membership, increase readership of The Naval Architect , expand specialist newsletter audiences and support wider business development activity. ‍ This is not a traditional hard-sell marketing role. RINA’s approach is “helpful first, selling second”: using high-quality content, audience insight and carefully targeted communications to build trust, engagement and long-term member value. ‍ The successful candidate will combine strong copywriting and campaign instincts with hands-on experience across CRM and email marketing. Maritime experience is not required, but curiosity, commercial instinct and an ability to engage credibly with technical subject matter are essential. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities include: ‍ ‍ Leading integrated email marketing andcommunications campaigns across membership, publications and businessdevelopment Managing CRM segmentation, automation andaudience targeting Creating and overseeing an organisation-wideemail communications calendar Supporting cross-sell and upsell campaigns andconversion journeys Working closely with content, events andpublications colleagues to maximise audience engagement Tracking campaign performance and usinginsight to improve effectiveness and conversion ‍ ‍ We are particularly interested in speaking with candidates fromintegrated communications, CRM, membership marketing, publishing, subscriptionor B2B content-led environments. ‍ RINA offers a highly flexible and collaborative working culture,with significant scope for creativity, experimentation and visible impact. ‍ Closing date: Friday 12 June Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so earlyapplications are encouraged. We will only be able to considerapplications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs,communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm,we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. item.title: Industry Relations Manager
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to be supporting a nationally recognised organisation at the forefront of one of the UK’s largest infrastructure and consumer engagement programmes. The Opportunity: ‍ This is a highly collaborative and externally focused role within the Corporate Affairs function, offering the opportunity to work closely with senior stakeholders across industry, regulation and partner organisations. ‍ The Industry Relations Manager will lead engagement activity with key industry partners, providing insight, coordination and strategic support across a range of programmes and initiatives. You will monitor sector developments, identify implications for stakeholders and support the delivery of collaborative projects that contribute to long-term organisational objectives. ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys building relationships, navigating complex stakeholder environments, and translating technical or policy information into clear, actionable insight. Main responsibilities: ‍ Build and maintain strong relationships with industry stakeholders, acting as a trusted point of contact. Support the development and delivery of stakeholder engagement strategies and collaborative projects. Monitor sector, regulatory and policy developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities. Produce clear briefings, presentations and insight to support internal decision-making. Work cross-functionally with communications, insights and corporate affairs teams to align stakeholder activity. Represent the organisation at external meetings, forums and industry events. Support and develop junior colleagues, helping ensure high-quality delivery across the function. ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience delivering stakeholder engagement or industry relations activity within a complex environment. Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including presenting complex information clearly. Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Ability to interpret policy, regulatory or operational developments and communicate implications effectively. Experience working within a regulated sector or complex stakeholder environment would be advantageous. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Tuesday2 nd June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ EllwoodAtfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications,policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of External Affairs
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with The Health Foundation on the appointment of a Head of External Affairs. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a high-profile opportunity to lead external affairs, media relations and stakeholder engagement for one of the UK’s most respected and influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting into the Deputy Director of Communications, the Head of External Affairs will lead a busy and high-performing team responsible for media, public affairs and stakeholder relations activity across the Foundation’s broad portfolio of work. ‍ The successful candidate will play a central role in shaping and delivering the Foundation’s influencing strategy - identifying opportunities to position the organisation at the forefront of political, policy and public debate across health and care. ‍ Working closely with senior leadership, the role will provide strategic counsel across media, government relations, reputational issues and stakeholder engagement, while helping ensure the Foundation’s work reaches and influences key audiences nationally. ‍ This role would suit an experienced communications, public affairs or external affairs leader who combines strong political and policy awareness with excellent judgement, leadership capability and highly developed stakeholder management skills. ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead the Foundation’s external affairs, media relations and influencing activity across health and public policy issues. Develop high-profile and timely opportunities to position the Foundation at the centre of policy, political and media debate. Oversee engagement with senior stakeholders across Westminster, Whitehall, media and the wider health sector. Lead and develop the Foundation’s media relations strategy, ensuring strong profile and impactful coverage across target audiences. Provide strategic communications and public affairs advice to senior leaders and colleagues across the organisation. Develop compelling messaging, briefings and external affairs materials for a range of audiences. Lead, motivate and develop ahigh-performing external affairs team. Contribute to the wider leadership of the Communications function as a senior member of the management team. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading external affairs, media, communications or public affairs functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of delivering profile, impact and organisational influence through integrated external affairs strategies. Excellent stakeholder management skills with the ability to build credibility at senior levels. Highly accomplished written and verbal communication skills. Experience leading and developing teams within fast-paced and high-profile environments. Ability to manage complex issues with sound judgement, discretion and political awareness. Interest in health policy, public policy and mission-led work. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy and advocacy. item.title: Deputy Director of Communications
item.description: ‍ ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Health Foundation on the appointment of a Deputy Director of Communications. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a significant senior leadership opportunity within one of the UK’s most respectedand influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Communications, the Deputy Director of Communications will play a central role in shaping and delivering integrated communications strategies across the Foundation’s major thematic programmes of work. ‍ The successful candidate will work closely with senior leadership across the organisation to ensure communications activity delivers impact, strengthens organisational influence and supports the Foundation’s ambition to be one of the leading voices in UK public policy. ‍ The remit is broad and highly strategic - spanning communications strategy, stakeholder engagement, external affairs, reputation, thought leadership and executive counsel. The role also sits on the Foundation’s Senior Leadership Team and plays an important role in the wider leadership of the Communications directorate. ‍ This opportunity would suit an experienced strategic communications leader with strong policy instincts, excellent leadership capability and experience operating within complex, high-profile or mission-led organisations. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead integrated communications strategies across major thematic programmes of work. Develop clear, compelling and influential messaging across a broad range of policy and organisational priorities. Work closely with senior colleagues to identify influencing, convening and stakeholder engagement opportunities. Provide strategic communications advice to the Chief Executive, directors and senior colleagues across the Foundation. Oversee communications planning and delivery across a range of high-profile projects and programmes. Represent the Foundation externally with media, political and stakeholder audiences. Lead and develop multidisciplinary communications teams. Contribute to the wider leadership and strategic direction of the Communications function and the Foundation more broadly. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading strategic communications functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of developing integrated communications strategies that deliver organisational impact and influence. Excellent stakeholder engagement and relationship-building skills. Strong written and verbal communication capability, including experience handling complex and sensitive issues. Experience advising senior leaders and operating at executive level. Leadership experience within fast-paced, high-profile or mission-led environments. Interest in health policy, health care and improving public outcomes. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.title: Senior Campaigns Officer
item.description: ‍ The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Campaigns Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Campaigns Manager and colleagues across policy, communications and public affairs, the Senior Campaigns Officer will support the delivery of integrated campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. ‍ The role will suit someone with strong political awareness, excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex policy issues into compelling campaign activity. You will thrive in a fast-paced environment, spotting opportunities to influence debate and helping deliver campaigns that achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present. ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Support the planning and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling campaign messaging and calls to action. Work collaboratively with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Support member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Monitor political, policy and media developments, identifying opportunities to advance the College’s position. Build productive relationships with external stakeholders and partner organisations. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to support continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Experience delivering campaigns, advocacy or influencing activity within policy, public affairs, healthcare, charities, membership bodies or related environments. Strong understanding of the UK political and health policy landscape. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop persuasive messaging for different audiences. Ability to translate complex policy or evidence into clear and engaging campaign activity. Strong organisational and project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities. Collaborative and confident working style with strong stakeholder engagement skills. Experience of digital campaigning tools or techniques would be beneficial. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Closing date: Friday 19th June ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.title: Campaigns Manager
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of a Campaigns Manager. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help shape and deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Head of Press and Campaigns, the Campaigns Manager will lead a small number of focused, strategic campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. The role will suit someone who combines strong political and strategic instincts with creativity, pace and excellent stakeholder management skills. ‍ You will play a key role in embedding a more modern, delivery-focused campaigning function, working collaboratively across policy, communications and public affairs to deliver campaigns that cut through and achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present [LINK HERE] ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Lead the development and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. item.title: Regulatory Affairs Lead
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield are proud to partner with Exchange for Change, the not-for-profit organisation appointed by the UK government to design, build and operate the UK's new Deposit Return Scheme (DRS) for single-use drinks containers made from PET plastic, steel and aluminium. ‍ This scheme which launches on 1st October 2027 across England, Scotland, Northern Ireland will be the world’s biggest DRS and one of the most ambitious environmental infrastructure programmes in the UK’s history. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ Exchange for Change is seeking a Regulatory Affairs Lead to take ownership of the organisation's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with DRS legislation and all statutory obligations. The role will be responsible for developing and executing a robust regulatory plan, while proactively monitoring the external environment to anticipate change and futureproof both Exchange for Change and the DRS. ‍ ‍ Central to the position is the ability to build and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with both with regulators and internal stakeholders, to drive engagement, confidence and transparency. The Regulatory Affairs Lead will play a key role in upholding the highest standards of governance and accountability, ensuring Exchange for Change operates with integrity at every level as it delivers one of the most significant environmental programmes in UK history. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Develop and implement Exchange for Change's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with UK DRS legislation while proactively monitoring the policy landscape to identify, assess and mitigate risks, and futureproof the organisation and the scheme. Lead Exchange for Change's input into external policy and regulatory consultations, representing the organisation at key stakeholder forums and shaping regulatory activity in line with strategic priorities. Act as the strategic lead for all regulator relationships, convening and directing cross-functional teams to deliver tailored engagement strategies across each nation, ensuring consistent messaging, coordinated approaches and effective intelligence-sharing. Develop and implement a proactive regulatory affairs engagement strategy, collaborating closely with the Head of External Affairs to align and streamline engagement with both regulators and government representatives. Oversee all regulatory reporting and compliance submissions, including quarterly compliance reports, annual funding reports and operational plans for England, Northern Ireland and Scotland. Recruit and line manage a Regulatory Affairs Manager, fostering a culture of collaboration, high performance and shared accountability across the regulatory affairs function. ‍ ‍ ‍ Experience required ‍ Extensive senior level experience in leading regulatory affairs, compliance or public affairs within a multi-stakeholder and matrixed organisation including FMCG, utilities, transport, logistics and environmental management. Strong experience in working with government departments, regulators and trade associations. Proven capability engaging diverse stakeholders across industry, supply chains, regulators and government. Strong understanding of devolved regulatory systems and differing policy/legislative contexts in England, Scotland and Northern Ireland. Strategic thinker who can assess, manage and mitigate sensitive issues/risks, balancing political, operational and commercial considerations effectively. Exceptional communication skills, capable of translating complex operational issues for political, industry and internal audiences. Understanding of UK environmental and waste management regulation would be beneficial. ‍ ‍ ‍ Benefits ‍ 34 days holiday (Including bank holidays) Car salary sacrifice scheme ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below by Monday 15th June, 2026 and include a copy of your CV. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.title: Communications Executive
item.description: ‍ About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield are proud to be supporting a leading transport and logistics company to recruit Communications Executive. The opportunity: ‍ Working as part of a collaborative communications team, you will support the planning, creation and delivery of content across a variety of channels. You will help ensure communications are clear, consistent and aligned with organisational priorities while contributing to initiatives that strengthen reputation and employee engagement. ‍ The successful candidate will be a strong communicator with excellent writing skills and the ability to tailor your approach for different audiences. You'll be organised, proactive and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ‍ Regular travel across the UK will be required to engage with colleagues, gather content and support communications activity across multiple locations. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Create and deliver engaging content across internal and external communications channels, including digital, social media and corporate platforms. Support the planning, coordination and delivery of communications campaigns and activities aligned to organisational priorities. Build relationships with colleagues across the business, travelling regularly across the UK to gather content, develop stories and support stakeholder engagement. Monitor media coverage and communications performance, providing insight and reporting to support informed decision-making. Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure consistent messaging and the timely delivery of communications materials, briefings and updates. ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience in communications, public relations, journalism, marketing or a related field. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Experience creating content for digital and social media channels. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Professional judgement and discretion when handling sensitive information. item.title: Senior Government Relations Manager
item.description: ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ Our client, a leading global technology company, seeks a Senior Government Relations Manager to join its small and highly effective UK public affairs team. You will work alongside senior leaders in the business to analyse and understand Government policies and maintain the organisation’s great reputation with politicians and other senior stakeholders. This role will support business objectives by helping to position the company as a trusted advisor to Government and policy-makers across a broad range of issues. ‍ ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Develop and execute the Government Relations programme, engaging with key political and industry influencers. Analyse Government policies and their potential impacts, providing constructive alternatives. Build and maintain relationships with Government, trade associations, and other senior stakeholders. Provide clear and concise briefings on policy developments and their implications. Coordinate internal communication on political monitoring and reporting. ‍ Key Skills and Experience ‍ In-depth understanding of the UK political process and policy development. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present complex ideas clearly. Experience in managing Government Affairs programmes in a large commercial organisation or public affairs agency. Strong relationship-building skills and ability to navigate multicultural environments. Experience of working in technology, telecoms or digital policy is highly desirable. ‍ Application ‍ This is a full-time position and we are particularly interested in hearing if you are immediately available or have a short notice period. Please apply by submitting your CV via the link below. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director of Campaigns
item.description: We are seeking an exceptional communicator to join our client’s Executive Leadership Team and lead the development of a bold, influential, and campaigns-led approach to advocacy, policy, and communications. ‍ This is a newly created leadership role with the opportunity to redefine how our client engages with Government, policy makers, the media, members, and key stakeholders. As Director of Campaigns, you will be responsible for ensuring the organisation is recognised as the authoritative and unified voice of the sector, driving meaningful policy outcomes and raising awareness of the industry's critical contribution to the UK economy. ‍ You will lead the integration of policy, public affairs, communications, and stakeholder engagement to deliver high-impact campaigns that influence decision-makers, strengthen reputation, and create lasting value for members. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, you will shape organisational strategy and drive a "campaigns first" culture across all advocacy activity. ‍ Working closely with colleagues you will create and deliver compelling campaigns that mobilise support, influence policy, and position the organisation at the centre of key national debates. ‍ This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced campaigns, public affairs, or policy leader looking to make a significant impact within one of the UK's most important sectors. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities include: ‍ Develop and deliver a strategic, insight-led campaigns programme that aligns with organisational priorities, drives member value, and delivers measurable policy and advocacy outcomes. Lead the development of evidence-based policy positions and thought leadership, translating complex issues into compelling narratives that influence political, regulatory, media, and industry audiences. Build and maintain influential relationships with Government, parliamentarians, regulators, policymakers, members, and industry stakeholders to strengthen the organisation's voice and impact. Enhance the organisation's external profile through strategic communications, media engagement, impactful storytelling, and acting as a senior spokesperson when required. Foster collaboration across membership, policy, communications, and external partners to maximise campaign engagement, amplify key messages, and strengthen sector-wide advocacy. Lead, develop, and inspire a high-performing team, ensuring effective resource management, accountability, innovation, and delivery against strategic objectives. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for a strategic and politically astute leader with a proven ability to deliver influential campaigns and shape public debate. ‍ Requirements include: ‍ Proven experience leading high-profile advocacy, public affairs, or stakeholder engagement campaigns, ideally within a trade association, membership organisation, professional body, or similarly complex stakeholder environment. Deep understanding of Government, Parliament, public policy, and political decision-making processes, with the ability to influence agendas and secure strategic outcomes. Exceptional communication, stakeholder engagement, and narrative-building skills, with the ability to translate complex issues into compelling and persuasive messaging. Strong media and external affairs experience, including acting as a credible spokesperson and building influential relationships across Government, media, regulatory, and industry networks. Demonstrable success in leading, developing, and inspiring high-performing teams within a fast-paced, results-driven environment. Commercially astute and strategically minded, with the credibility, judgement, and leadership capability to operate effectively at Executive Leadership Team level and build consensus across diverse stakeholder groups. ‍ ‍ This is a rare opportunity to build and lead a newly created function with significant strategic influence. You will help shape the future of an industry that underpins the UK's economy, leading campaigns that drive real change and ensure the sector's voice is heard where it matters most. ‍ If you are an ambitious and influential leader with a passion for advocacy, policy, and strategic communications, we would love to hear from you. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information and to apply, please share a copy of your CV via the form below. ‍ Deadline for applications is midday on Thursday 2nd July. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading membership body within the health and social sciences sector. ‍ The opportunity: ‍ As Digital Marketing Specialist, you will play a key role in delivering and optimising digital marketing activity across paid media and email marketing. This is a technical marketing role which combines PPC, paid social, email campaigns and marketing automation, with responsibility for driving brand awareness, audience engagement and conversion across a range of products and services. You will manage Google Ads activity, including Google Ad Grants, oversee paid social campaigns across multiple platforms, and work closely with agency partners to ensure strong performance and return on investment. You will use data and insight to inform decisions, track campaign performance, and report on results to identify opportunities for continuous improvement. You will contribute to email marketing campaigns, customer journeys and content creation, working collaboratively with colleagues across the organisation. This is an excellent opportunity for a digitally savvy marketer to make a visible impact within a purpose-driven organisation while helping to shape and develop its digital marketing capabilities. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Plan, deliver and optimise PPC and paid social campaigns across Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Conduct audience targeting, keyword research, A/B testing and campaign optimisation to improve engagement and conversion. Set up tracking and reporting using GA4 and other analytics tools, providing actionable insights and recommendations. Create and deliver email marketing campaigns, automated customer journeys and compelling digital content, while collaborating with stakeholders on wider marketing initiatives. Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to support integrated marketing campaigns and drive audience engagement. Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven experience managing PPC and paid social campaigns across platforms including Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Strong knowledge of digital marketing analytics, conversion tracking, campaign reporting and performance optimisation. Experience delivering email marketing campaigns, automation journeys and audience segmentation strategies. Solid understanding of SEO, CRO, digital best practice, GDPR compliance and emerging marketing technologies. Highly analytical and commercially minded, with excellent communication, copywriting and stakeholder management skills. How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ The deadline for applications is Monday 26th June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them.
